refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated capability to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context (site conditions, task scope, safety constraints), record derived requirements with traceability to source inputs, and maintain a managed baseline history including calibration updates and performance target revisions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional concerns: derivation (analysis/synthesis), recording (data capture), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into analyzable sub-functions while maintaining the critical traceability and baseline management aspects. High safety criticality demands explicit linkage between worksite inputs and performance targets to enable safety-critical verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall demonstrate: (1) a documented derivation method that maps worksite mission inputs to performance targets with explicit rationale; (2) a recorded baseline with full traceability to source inputs and acceptance criteria; (3) a managed history showing all calibration/revision events with timestamps, actors, and change justification; (4) independent audit of traceability chain from worksite context to performance acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task scope, safety constraints, equipment capabilities).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with explicit traceability to source worksite inputs and acceptance criteria.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance requirements including all calibration updates, revisions, and approval records.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Provide performance requirement data to operational planning and safety assessment functions via standardized interfaces.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance requirements management system (PRMS) that ingests worksite mission context via structured input forms, applies a rule-based derivation engine to map inputs to performance targets, stores requirements in a versioned database with full audit trail, and exposes requirements via API to downstream consumers.

DP-B: Distributed performance derivation where each operational subsystem (vehicle, teleoperation, safety services) maintains its own performance requirement derivation logic, with a central registry that aggregates and reconciles derived targets.

DP-C: Manual performance requirement derivation by Site Manager with template-based recording and centralized baseline management, supported by a lightweight database for history tracking and change control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance requirements management system (PRMS) with rule-based derivation engine, versioned requirement storage, and API-based exposure to operational planning and safety assessment.

potential_sources: PRMS derivation logic is decoupled from operational execution (vehicle, teleoperation, safety services) via API interfaces; changes to derivation rules do not require changes to operational modules.
Requirement recording and baseline management are co-located in PRMS, reducing coupling between data capture and lifecycle control.
Traceability linkage (worksite inputs → performance targets) is maintained within PRMS schema, avoiding distributed traceability logic.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because the derivation engine is isolated from operational subsystems and accessed only via well-defined interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry). The centralized approach reduces the risk of inconsistent requirement interpretation across distributed subsystems.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured input schema (site conditions, task scope, safety constraints) that is stable across mission types.
ASSUMPTION: A rule-based derivation engine can be developed that reliably maps worksite inputs to performance targets with sufficient accuracy for safety-critical operations.
RISK: If worksite input schema is incomplete or evolves frequently, the derivation engine may produce invalid or incomplete performance targets, compromising safety verification.
RISK: If the rule-based engine is opaque or difficult to audit, traceability verification may be infeasible, violating the acceptance criterion for documented traceability.
RISK: High safety criticality requires that the derivation logic itself be subject to rigorous verification and validation; this may increase development cost and schedule.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Recommend that DP-A be refined to explicitly include: (1) a formal specification of the worksite input schema with validation rules; (2) a documented derivation rule set with rationale for each rule; (3) an audit trail mechanism that records all derivation steps and intermediate results; (4) a verification protocol that validates derived performance targets against worksite mission context and safety constraints. These refinements will support the high safety criticality and traceability acceptance criteria.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and provides enterprise-level capabilities including performance requirements management, mission planning, and system-wide coordination. The PRMS (DP-A) is realized as a core capability within the NGHE Solution module, responsible for deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements that are consumed by Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives worksite mission context (task scope, site conditions, operational constraints) from Site Manager via Tasking interface; this input drives performance requirement derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exposes derived performance requirements and baseline history to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems via Telemetry interface for operational planning and safety assessment.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and worksite safety plan inputs via SafetyAlert interface (in); provides performance requirements that incorporate safety constraints to Safety Services subsystem (out).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for communication with remote Site Manager and Tele-operator for mission context updates and performance requirement queries.

module_containment_scores.notes: PRMS is a core enterprise capability that must be centralized at the NGHE Solution level to serve all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with consistent performance requirements. High containment score reflects that performance requirement derivation, recording, and management are logically cohesive and should not be distributed. Low diffusion score indicates minimal risk of requirement logic leaking into subsystem modules.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing tasks within derived performance targets, not for deriving or managing requirements. Assigning PRMS to Vehicle would create high diffusion (requirement logic scattered across vehicle subsystems) and violate separation of concerns. Vehicle should consume performance requirements from NGHE Solution, not generate them.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and monitoring, not for performance requirement derivation. Assigning PRMS to Teleoperation would create high diffusion and coupling between teleoperation logic and requirement management. Teleoperation should consume performance requirements from NGHE Solution for remote task planning.

Safety Services module is responsible for safety monitoring and enforcement, not for performance requirement derivation. While Safety Services consumes performance requirements (to assess safety compliance), it should not own the derivation logic. Assigning PRMS to Safety Services would create high diffusion and conflate safety assessment with requirement management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Performance requirement derivation, recording, and management are enterprise-level capabilities that must be centralized at the NGHE Solution module to ensure consistency, traceability, and auditability across all operational subsystems. The PRMS (DP-A) is logically cohesive and should not be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules. This containment strategy supports the high safety criticality and traceability acceptance criteria by maintaining a single source of truth for performance requirements.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module becomes a bottleneck for performance requirement updates, operational responsiveness may be degraded. Mitigation: Implement efficient API-based access to PRMS with caching and asynchronous update mechanisms.

RISK: If PRMS is tightly coupled to NGHE Solution's core mission planning logic, changes to requirement derivation rules may require changes to mission planning. Mitigation: Design PRMS as a loosely coupled service with well-defined interfaces.

RISK: If NGHE Solution module is geographically distributed (e.g., cloud-based vs. on-site), network latency may impact performance requirement derivation and update cycles. Mitigation: Implement local caching and offline derivation capabilities with eventual consistency.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Recommend that the NGHE Solution module be further decomposed to isolate the PRMS as a distinct sub-module with clear ownership, interface contracts, and performance SLAs. This will support future scalability and enable independent testing/verification of the performance requirement derivation logic. Additionally, recommend that the Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces be refined to specify the exact schema and validation rules for worksite mission context and safety constraints, ensuring that PRMS can reliably derive performance targets.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized PRMS with rule-based derivation engine) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) derives performance requirements from worksite mission context via rule-based engine; (2) records requirements with traceability via versioned database; (3) maintains baseline history via audit trail; (4) exposes requirements via API. The DP is logically sound and exhibits low coupling. However, the DP assumes that a reliable rule-based derivation engine can be developed; this assumption must be validated during detailed design.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK: If PRMS implementation is scattered across multiple NGHE Solution sub-modules (e.g., requirement derivation in one sub-module, recording in another, baseline management in a third), the logical cohesion of DP-A will be lost. Mitigation: Ensure PRMS is realized as a single, integrated sub-module within NGHE Solution with clear ownership and interface contracts.

DIFFUSION RISK: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules implement local copies of the derivation logic (e.g., for offline operation or performance optimization), requirement inconsistency and traceability loss will occur. Mitigation: Enforce that all performance requirement derivation is performed by NGHE Solution's PRMS; subsystems consume requirements via API only.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for PRMS because it is the only module with visibility to all operational subsystems and worksite mission context. The module containment scores confirm that NGHE Solution has the highest containment score (95) and lowest diffusion score (5) compared to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The assignment is architecturally sound and supports the traceability and baseline management acceptance criteria.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible via: (1) inspection of PRMS derivation rule set and worksite input schema; (2) traceability analysis from worksite mission context to performance targets; (3) audit trail review of baseline history and calibration updates; (4) functional testing of PRMS with representative worksite scenarios; (5) independent safety assessment of derived performance targets. The centralized PRMS (IM) enables efficient verification because all requirement logic is co-located and auditable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. The PRMS is a core capability of NGHE Solution that must be realized before Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services can be operationalized. The sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.4) are allocated to specific PRMS functions (derivation, recording, management, exposure) that can be independently designed and verified.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Refine the Tasking interface specification to define the exact schema for worksite mission context (site conditions, task scope, operational constraints) and validation rules. This will enable PRMS to reliably derive performance targets.

REVISION 2: Refine the SafetyAlert interface specification to define the exact schema for safety constraints and worksite safety plan inputs. This will ensure that PRMS incorporates safety requirements into derived performance targets.

REVISION 3: Develop a formal specification of the PRMS derivation rule set with rationale for each rule. This will support verification and validation of the rule-based engine and enable independent safety assessment.

REVISION 4: Define performance SLAs for PRMS (e.g., requirement derivation latency, baseline update frequency, API response time) to ensure that NGHE Solution can meet operational responsiveness requirements.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 has been successfully refined into four atomic sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.4) that are logically cohesive and independently verifiable. DP-A (centralized PRMS) is a sound design solution that exhibits low coupling and high containment within NGHE Solution. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) have passed. The next level of decomposition should focus on the four sub-FRs to derive detailed design parameters and implementation modules for: (1) worksite mission context derivation logic; (2) requirement recording and traceability mechanisms; (3) baseline history and change control; (4) API-based requirement exposure. This decomposition will enable detailed design and verification of the PRMS capability.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task scope, safety constraints, equipment capabilities).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with explicit traceability to source worksite inputs and acceptance criteria.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance requirements including all calibration updates, revisions, and approval records.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Provide performance requirement data to operational planning and safety assessment functions via standardized interfaces.

assumptions: ASSUMPTION-1: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured input schema (site conditions, task scope, safety constraints) that is stable across mission types and can be validated by PRMS.
ASSUMPTION-2: A rule-based derivation engine can be developed that reliably maps worksite inputs to performance targets with sufficient accuracy for safety-critical operations.
ASSUMPTION-3: The Tasking and SafetyAlert interfaces will be refined to specify exact schemas and validation rules for worksite mission context and safety constraints.
ASSUMPTION-4: NGHE Solution has sufficient computational and storage capacity to host the PRMS and serve all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) with acceptable latency.
ASSUMPTION-5: Site Manager and Tele-operator will provide accurate and complete worksite mission context inputs to PRMS via the Tasking interface.
open_risks: RISK-1: If the rule-based derivation engine is opaque or difficult to audit, traceability verification may be infeasible, violating the acceptance criterion for documented traceability. MITIGATION: Develop a formal specification of the derivation rule set with rationale for each rule; implement audit trail mechanism that records all derivation steps.

RISK-2: If worksite input schema is incomplete or evolves frequently, the derivation engine may produce invalid or incomplete performance targets, compromising safety verification. MITIGATION: Conduct worksite mission context analysis to identify all required input parameters; implement schema versioning and validation mechanisms.

RISK-3: If PRMS becomes a bottleneck for performance requirement updates, operational responsiveness may be degraded. MITIGATION: Implement efficient API-based access with caching and asynchronous update mechanisms; define performance SLAs for PRMS.

RISK-4: High safety criticality requires that the derivation logic itself be subject to rigorous verification and validation; this may increase development cost and schedule. MITIGATION: Plan for independent safety assessment and formal verification of the derivation rule set; allocate sufficient resources for V&V activities.
evidence_needed: EVIDENCE-1: Formal specification of worksite mission context input schema with validation rules and examples.
EVIDENCE-2: Documented derivation rule set with rationale for each rule and traceability to safety requirements.
EVIDENCE-3: PRMS audit trail mechanism design and implementation that records all derivation steps and intermediate results.
EVIDENCE-4: Functional test cases for PRMS with representative worksite scenarios and expected performance targets.
EVIDENCE-5: Independent safety assessment report confirming that derived performance targets are consistent with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
EVIDENCE-6: Traceability matrix linking worksite mission context inputs to derived performance targets and acceptance criteria.
EVIDENCE-7: Performance SLA specification for PRMS (derivation latency, baseline update frequency, API response time).
